rows.374 Pittsfield Rd. Lenox MA:
Carolyn, in case you haven't heard yet we've been awarded the Lenox dealerships with CM&B. Could you please help me with filling this out again? Not sure if we can just use the same one we have already sent or if there is anything specific with the document 

Ryan P Gilligan, VP Field Operations
Refrigerated Structures of New England
155 Millennium Circle, Suite 104
Lakeville, MA 02347

As discussed, I went there today.   
One of the Anthony glass doors on the Heineken cooler needs a couple of repair parts (which we might have in the shop).   I’ll ping Doug to look for some and if we don’t have the parts we will have to order them from Anthony. 

Whoever does the repair will have to know how to “fix” Anthony glass doors.    I know that Doug and Zach do.  Not sure who else does.   It’s pretty simple stuff unless you’ve never seen it before.  

This repair should be invoiced on a T&M basis.   AND it should only take one experienced tech about 2hrs plus travel time to Attleboro.

Thanks,
G

57 Magazine ST. Roxbury MA 
67 Kemble ST. Boston Panel delivery 5/30/25

City Fresh is supposedly signing lease papers this week.   Once that’s done, we will get a deposit check.  The only thing holding us up at that point will be some plumbing work that has to be completed prior to us going in.  We are going to use the 5” panels that came back from Western Mass

We are working with a new system but you should see the Subcontract shortly for Hyannis. There is not going to be a Subcontract for Randolph, you can invoice that as a regular invoice, it is not a remodel.  I appreciate you checking 🙂

Carolyn/Ryan,

I just got a note from Billy at VERTEC.   He asked us if we could start the Randolph ceiling on Tuesday, May 6th.   (We have it currently starting on the 5th)

They need to demo the ceiling on Monday night.    I told him that we could BUT that we also still needed confirmation that we could install during day shift.  

He’s verifying that.  

So please adjust the schedule.    I still don’t have that functionality yet in Knowify.   Gabe is working on it.

I just got off the phone with Billy Faradie, who had just got off the phone with Mark Bermingham.

According to Billy, the conversation went like this:
Mark              “Billy have you locked in RSNE for the work at Hyannis and Randolph?”
Billy                “No Mark, I haven’t sent you the quotes yet.”
Mark              “I don’t care.  These are safety issues.   They need to get done ASAP.  Call Greg and get them scheduled”

Sooooo, Billy told me these are both approved and they will get POs to us shortly.   He also wants to know how soon we can start on them.    I told him Hyannis is the worst of the two and should be first priority.  We will schedule Randolph immediately after.  I told him that I’d give him a tentative start date tomorrow after I confirmed material availability.

To provide an answer to Vertec, the info I need now is:   
Do we have enough panels for both of these jobs and if not, when will we have it.

Hyannis Meat Cooler will need about fifty 10 footers
Randolph Meat Prep Ceiling will need  a dozen 14 footers and a dozen 12s.

Hi Joe,
I have signed the quote and submitted.  We will cut the deposit check and get that out today.  This job is an emergency and needs to be done as soon as possible.  Could you send an ETA when you can?
Thank you!
Christine
 
50 Years in business
Over 200 years of experience

90 First Street
Bridgewater, MA  02324
508-697-5000

know the glass doors for Lynn’s Variety have been ordered.    
I spoke with the GC today.   
They are targeting to be ready for us to install the box during the week of 6/23.   
We can build the box that week, leaving dead men in the glass door openings and install the doors when we received them.
Mary Cobleigh wants 7/14 start and 2 nights

I’ve had several conversations with Chris Hawes today regarding the heights of the boxes at TitS-Bedford.   The keg cooler wall height is going to stay at 8’ because he can’t get refrigeration equipment in time for 10’ walls.   Also we are going to have to possibly reduce the wall height a few inches for the cooler due to a duct that may or may not be in the way.   We’ll have to check that when we get there.      Bottom line is we will charge them for 10’ers.

Since I’m the only one who’s seen this job, I’ll plan on heading up there on Monday morning to get the crew started up.   Have we identified the crew for these boxes?

Hey all,
I spoke with Chris Hawes (Harbour Food Services) and he confirmed that we got the Tavern – Bedford job.   
They are going with the 10’ walls on the Kitchen facing side so they can attach a drop ceiling.   
I’ve got a revision completed.  The new price is $71,773.
 
Ryan,
I’ll connect with Randy Gaspar (Site Super for JJ Welch) and see when he wants us up there.   And I’ll check to see when we can get materials up there…. He thought he’d have the floor work completed in the next week or so.

Hi Eric 
We would like to install both doors in one day and leave the small door closed in the meantime, You can get the electrical wired to the doors and we can come back and program them both.
Hi Joe, we can't do that because the smaller door has a portable AC unit cut into the wall to provide temporary cooling. It's already challenging enough sequencing the installs in the middle of our busiest time of year and I can't make the door install contingent on refrigeration install.

Afternoon Carolyn,

Since we're in July now we're at peak production and we have no availability next week. We can tweak our schedule to allow for the installation of the larger of the 2 doors on Monday 7/14, but the smaller door opening can't be cut and installed until we have refrigeration online which is still TBD.

Please confirm that works for you guys and we'll go from there.

Thanks and Happy 4th

I spoke with Jeff Sullivan this morning.   
He just got off the phone with the folks at Acushnet Schools.

As far as they are concerned, this project is approved.   They are now just waiting for Trane to get their sh*t together and get the POs out.    

I still don’t think we should fabricate anything yet BUT we do need to be prepared to have panels available.   I’m hoping that we can start by the week of 7/21.

Thanks,
Greg Watka

Hi all,

The attached request is for the Acushnet Schools projects, which we have quoted to B&G.   B&G then quoted to Trane, who will be “running” the project.    If we get this work, it will be completed this summer.

They are asking for a lot of info BUT I think we can/should provide COIs, OSHA 300A’s, and our health and safety docs.    As for the other stuff:   I don’t know what an EMR is.  We haven’t had any OSHA citations.   Also not sure what their looking for with Bond Capacity.

Thanks,

All,

I spoke to Jeff Sullivan (B&G Restaurant Supply) this afternoon.    I asked him for some tentative dates so we could get them on the calendar.    

He told me that the project was “confirmed” but that he had not received a PO from Trane.    He went on to say that a final approval was needed from the school committee, who was meeting on June 2nd.     

I wouldn’t build any doors, floors, etc. until we get a PO from B&G.

This is the last note I have for West Roxbury VA.   I reached out today to Chris Greeley on a question regarding Brockton but I also asked him if they were ready for us in WR next week.   
He said Paige was out for the rest of the week but he would get back to us by Friday.  

The only reason that we shouldn’t be able to get in there to start on Monday is IF cooler #1 and/or #3 has not been emptied out by the VA.

Cooler #2 demo and rebuild should be last because there’s prep work(electrical/refrigeration) needed before we get in there.
ompleted Salad Cooler Box and Door 5/21/25

Hi Guys just following up on the next cooler install. We have 2 dates that would be good to start.
The week of February 24th through the 28th  or the week of March 3rd through the 7th. The earlier date would be preferable. So we can get the epoxy floor in the cooler earlier.

Let us know what is good for you.

In this trip we would like to install the deli cooler and the freezer in the grocery storage..

Thank You

3 CLR DOORS AND 1 FZR DOOR
Brian Avery - union job.

RSNE is going on the road baby!!!!! Woooooohoooooooo! 

But first, insurance….. 

I saw the email with the COI for the DAG Ohio group which made me realize we will need OH worker’s comp. I spoke to Laurie (insurance broker) about it, and she said OH is a PIA about WC but she is looking into it for us. 

I’m assuming that the panels will be delivered to the jobsite. Laurie wants to know if we want to get insurance for the panels when they are on the jobsite with something called installation coverage. She said to think about how long they will be hanging out there before we install them, where they will be stored/how they will be protected. She said to look at the contract with DAG and see if there is language about when the transfer of ownership of the panels happens. We could potentially be responsible for the panels until project completion, or maybe it will be until the GC signs off. 

Also, if we are using sub labor, she said we have to get temp agency to provide us proof of WC in Ohio.
Bob Chase
